entry.title: S/4 HANA - different PRT behaviour in Fiori applications
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are having some issues with Fiori aopplications usied in EAM/PM/AM processes. We are experiencing different behaviour depending on which Fiori application we use to access maintenance order data. Focus is on management of production resources and tools (PRT).

We are wonding if anyone has had a similar issue and if we are perhaps missing some configuration. These are all standard applications without any enhancments.

1. APP Perform Maintenance Job - in the same row SAP states that there are no PRT while the total PRT count is 4

2. APP Manage Maintenance Orders - documents are not shown as PRT and it is not possible to add them in the order - the category D is miissing from dropdown list

3. APP Maintenance orders - all PRT are displayed correctly

Best regards, M.

entry.title: ‌🇧🇷‌ Tax Summit 2026
entry.content:
Tax Summit 2026 brings together tax leaders and specialists from across Brazil for two days of focused discussions on the future of taxation. The event provides a forum to examine regulatory change, explore emerging practices, and discuss how organizations can respond to a rapidly evolving tax environment.

Brazil’s tax reform introduces fundamental changes to tax calculation and control. These changes demand tighter integration with ERP systems and a higher standard of data quality. Approaches that rely primarily on downstream reporting are increasingly exposed to inconsistencies, manual rework, and governance challenges.

Featured session: April 1, 12:20-12:50 pm
Tax Reform in practice: How SAP Document and Reporting Compliance and ophera.fiscal are redefining tax compliance in Brazil

This session looks at how an integrated approach to tax compliance helps organizations adapt to the new regulatory model. The speakers will explain how SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), in combination with ophera.fiscal, enables closer integration with ERP processes, supports assisted tax calculation, and strengthens governance of tax data.

This session looks at how an integrated approach to tax compliance helps organizations adapt to the new regulatory model. The speakers will explain how DRC, in combination with ophera.fiscal, enables closer integration with ERP processes, supports assisted tax calculation, and strengthens governance of tax data.

Speakers

Eduardo Kyono, Product Owner, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, SAP
João Malfará, SAP Document and Reporting Compliance Consultant, Phoron do Brasil 

The event will be held in Portuguese.

Check out the agenda and register HERE.

Are you interested in learning more about Tax Reform and SAP Document and Reporting Compliance from SAP experts? Join the following Work Zone groups to stay up to date:
  • Brazil - Tax Reform Localization
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (EFD-REINF)
  • Brazil - SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (NF-e)

entry.title: BDC : BW Modernization - Key to Innovation for SAP Customers
entry.content:
Introduction :

BW Modernization (SAP BW Modernization) is the process of upgrading and transforming your existing SAP BW system into a modern, flexible, cloud-ready analytics platform.

It Means :

  • Moving from old SAP BW systems (BW 3.x, 7.x) to modern platforms like:
  • BW/4HANA
  • SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)
  • SAP Datasphere
  • Shifting from traditional data warehousing to real-time, cloud-based analytics

 

Modernize : 

Why Customer Feels BW Modernization is Difficult ?

  • Manual code remediation (ABAP, transformations)
  • Dependency mapping is complex
  • Risk of breaking existing reports
  • Time-consuming (months/years)
  • Lack of skilled BW resources

Customer Feels :
“Transformation is not just technical — it’s risky and slow.”

  • Customers fear modernization because of risk
  • Too many objects → Info Cubes, DSO, queries
  • This is where AI changes the gam

Best Approach for BW Modernization :

Lift :

Shift: 

Innovate : 

How AI-Assists 

Business Value to our Customers :

  • 50–70% faster modernization (with AI)
  • Reduced cost & effort
  • Real-time insights
  • AI-driven decision-making
  • Future-ready architecture

 “Modernizing BW is no longer a project — it’s an intelligent transformation.”

Outcome :

  • Focus on value, not technology
  • Faster ROI
  • Better decisions
  • Competitive advantage

 

Thank you!

Regards,

Giri

entry.title: Re: Implementing Custom Logic to Include Project Informat...
entry.content: Hi Chris, Thank you for your quick response. For your information, I have already reviewed the provided links and implemented the solution as described. Based on the Business Scenario | SAP Help Portal, I followed the below steps: Created an extension field for the Billing Document and extended the email template Forwarded the extension field from Sales to Billing Created a Custom CDS View Populated the extension field for the Billing Output Header (via BAdI implementation) Regarding step 4, I created custom logic "YY1_SALESHEADERMODIFICATION" with the following: Affected Business Context: SD_SALESDOC BAdI: Modification of Header Custom Fields in Sales Documents (SD_SLS_MODIFY_HEAD) I also implemented the code as referenced in step 4.6. Additionally, I extended our existing billing document email template (based on the standard template "SD_BD_DEFAULT_EMAIL_TEMPLATE") to include the field: {{YY1_CustomerProjectID_BDH}} Testing Performed Created a sales order and generated a billing document Triggered the email output Issue Observed The email output still shows: "000000000000000000000000" instead of the expected value. I further analyzed the issue and modified the logic to read data from I_SalesDocumentItem. When tested within the Custom Logic app, it returned the correct values. However, the email output continues to display the same incorrect value. Current Understanding It appears that the custom logic implemented under SD_SALESDOC is not being triggered during the email generation process. From the documentation of the affected business context, it states that this BAdI is used for modifying sales document header extension fields, but it does not explicitly confirm its usage for email template extensions. In contrast, for print forms, the documentation for: Header Modification in Standard Output Form for Billing Documents (SD_BIL_PRINT_STANDARD_HEAD) clearly specifies that custom fields for form templates should be handled there. I also reviewed the following documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/0b6ec00b397349b09ec69ec7e60c2667.html?locale=en-US This explains how to update customer invoices but does not clearly indicate where to implement logic specifically for billing email template extensions. Request Could you please advise on the correct extension point or BAdI that should be used to populate custom fields in the billing email template? This field is critical for our business, as the Project ID / Project Description must be included in the invoice email sent to customers. If needed, I can share detailed screenshots and documentation of the development and testing performed. Please let me know the best way to provide these. Thanks in advance for your guidance.

entry.title: Beyond Row-Level Security: Implementing Widget-Level Custom Authorization in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
The Challenge: One Dashboard to Rule Them All (But Not Show Them Everything)

We’ve all been there. The Board of Directors requests a "Management Dashboard". They want a 360-degree view of the company: Production efficiency, HR turnover, Finance liquidity, and Sales KPIs all in one place.

But here is the catch: While the CEO wants to see everything, the Head of Production shouldn't be distracted by sensitive HR Payroll charts, and the Sales Lead doesn't need to see the Finance department's deep-dive debt ratios.

The Problem: Standard Row-Level Security (RLS) only filters the data inside the charts. If a user isn't authorized, they often see a "No Data" ghost-chart or a broken visualization. This ruins the Executive Experience.

The Goal: If you aren't authorized to see the data, the chart shouldn't even exist on your screen, or in other words, it should not render.

In SAC, this is achieved by bridging the gap between the User Profile and the Canvas Scripting Engine. We aren't just securing data; we are orchestrating the user interface.

Prerequisites for Success

Before we dive into the "How-To," ensure you have the following in place:

  1. User Mapping Table: A central source (could be an acquired CSV or an import data from any system) that maps User IDs to their respective Functional Areas/KPIs.

  2. Consistent Naming Conventions: Ensure your charts are named logically in the Outline (e.g., Chart_Inv_Units, Chart_Fin_Revenue, Chart_Units_Sold) to make your scripts maintainable.
  3. Dashboard with KPIs visualized: Have a dashboard ready with some charts in it. (In this example., Revenue, Units Sold, and Units in the Inventory are the numeric KPI tiles.)

The image shows you the charts in the dashboard with their respective names.

Note: Having all the Charts/Widgets in a flow layout panel helps the flexibility of adjusting the position of the widgets based on the visibility of other widgets.

The Solution: Step-by-Step Implementation

1. Defining the Authorization Matrix

Start by creating a specialized model that defines who sees what. This acts as our "Source of Truth" for the UI. 

In this use case, I use Excel as an authorization matrix.

user_idkpi_tiledummy
GE285115rev1
GE283973uni1
GE283973invuni1
GE285115uni1

From the table, we get to know that the user GE285115 can see only "rev" and "uni" kpis, but not the "invuni". And the user GE283973 can see "uni" and "invuni" kpis, but not the "rev" kpi.

Now, using this table from Excel or from any system, create an import model that looks exactly like this.

2. The onInitialization Scripting Magic

The heart of this solution lies in the onInitialization event. When the dashboard loads, we don't wait for the user to click anything. We programmatically "audit" the user and hide unauthorized components.

Before implementing the code in the onInitialization, create a table using the Auth model that was created in the previous step with the following fields in it, and make it hidden by default.

Here's the code for our example:

Application.showBusyIndicator("Applying Visual level authorization...");
var user_id = Application.getUserInfo();
var i = 0;
Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter("user_id",user_id.id);
var result = Table_1.getDataSource().getResultSet();
console.log(result);
for(i=0;i<result.length;i++){
	if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="rev")
	{
		Chart_Fin_Revenue.setVisible(true);
	}
	else if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="uni")
	{
		Chart_Units_Sold.setVisible(true);
	}
	else if(result[i]["kpi_tile"].id==="invuni")
	{
		Chart_Inv_Units.setVisible(true);
	} // Add else if incase if you have additional kpis
}
Application.hideBusyIndicator();

And make the default visibility of each chart hidden, as shown below.

3. Testing

Logged in as GE283973 (Assumption: This user should see only "Units Sold" and "Units in Inventory" KPI)

Logged in as GE285115 (Assumption: This user should see only "Units Sold" and "Revenue" KPI)

Lifecycle & Governance:

Maintaining your security matrix shouldn’t be a headache. Whether you’re uploading a flat file or scheduling a job from a source system to the auth model, the admin or the model owner has to maintain it. Always set your import method to "Clean and replace selected version of data." This ensures that as your organizational structure evolves, your dashboard security reflects the current reality, not yesterday’s permissions.

Why this matters for the Enterprise

By implementing Visualisation Level Security, you provide:

  • Reduced Cognitive Load: Executives only see what is actionable for them.
  • Clean Authorization: Instead of showing 'No Data' errors or broken charts, the UI dynamically hides unauthorized KPIs, maintaining a professional and secure dashboard layout.
  • Scalability: You maintain one dashboard instead of five different versions for five different departments.

Pro tip: One can pause the refresh for the widgets that are invisible to the users to enhance the dashboard's performance.

I hope this guide helps you elevate your next SAC project from a standard report to a premium executive experience. Have you run into similar challenges with UI-level security? If you have questions or hit a snag during your implementation, feel free to drop a comment below. I’d love to troubleshoot with you and hear your thoughts!

Happy Learning!!!

Vijay Bhaskaran

entry.title: Sending Dynamic Slack Notifications from SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) Using Groovy Script
entry.content:
Introduction

In modern integration scenarios, real-time notifications play a crucial role in keeping teams informed about system events such as deployments, alerts, or failures.

One effective way to achieve this is by integrating SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) with collaboration tools like Slack.

In this blog, we will build an iFlow that dynamically sends Slack notifications using Groovy scripts, based on user-specific data received in a JSON payload.

Use Case / Scenario

Consider a scenario where:

  • A system sends a JSON payload containing multiple users
  • Each user needs to receive a personalized Slack notification
  • The CPI iFlow processes this payload and sends messages individually

Sample JSON Payload

iFlow Design Overview

The integration flow consists of the following steps:

  • HTTPS Sender Adapter (triggered via Postman)
  • Groovy Script (Extract common properties)
  • JSON to XML Converter
  • General Splitter (split users)
  • Groovy Script (build Slack message)
  • Open Connectors Adapter (send to Slack)

Step 1: Extract Properties Using Groovy Script

  • This script extracts common fields like priority and reference and stores them as message properties.

Step 2: JSON to XML Conversion

  • The JSON payload is converted into XML format
  • This enables XPath usage in the splitter step

Step 3: General Splitter Configuration

  • The General Splitter splits the payload into individual users
  • Each user is processed separately
  • XPath Example: /users

Step 4: Build Slack Message Using Groovy Script

  • This script dynamically constructs a Slack message for each user.

Step 5: Send Notification to Slack

Use Open Connectors Adapter

Configure Slack connector with:

  • Channel name
  • Authentication
  • Message body

Testing Using Postman

  • Send a POST request to CPI endpoint
  • Use the sample JSON payload
  • Trigger the iFlow
  • Here I used Students details as example JSON payload

Slack Output (Expected Result)

Each user will receive a message like:

 Key Benefits

  • Dynamic message generation
  • Handles multiple users efficiently
  • Real-time Slack notifications
  • Reusable Groovy scripts

Challenges Faced

During implementation, a few challenges were addressed:

  • Handling null values in payload
  • Ensuring proper JSON parsing
  • Formatting Slack messages dynamically
  • Managing splitter configuration correctly

Conclusion

Using SAP Cloud Integration with Groovy scripting, we can easily build scalable and dynamic notification systems.

This approach is highly useful for:

  • Deployment notifications
  • Alerting systems
  • Monitoring integrations

It enhances visibility and ensures teams stay updated in real time.

#SAPCPI #SAPIntegrationSuite #Groovy #SlackIntegration #Automation

 @Souravraj_Maitra

@Bibekananda1231

entry.title: Re: Maintain PIR app and splitting indicator in S4 Hana p...
entry.content:
Good day @srinivasareddybhimanadham 

Thank you for your question.

Question 1: How to load the forecast on weekly basis ? excel upload we have option for only monthly.

Answer: Based on my understanding of what you intend to achieve, which is not use Excel because it allows you to only upload monthly forecast. Therefore, my suggestion would be for you to use weekly PIRs directly, it requires the material to have Weekly as forecast period indicator. In that case, Maintain PIRs supports weekly maintenance with 52 weekly periods.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/03c2dd11c91f4df18e65ea1886dd54e0.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/71b373b9418746c09a9e8bf0123cc958.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 2: unable to find Automatic splitting indicator of monthly loaded forecast into weekly, how to achieve this in S4 public cloud, any workaround solution for this?

Answer: You are trying to carry out a Public Cloud equivalent of the old plant level splitting indicator customising from R/3.  The practical workaround in Public Cloud is either: Direct weekly PIR maintenance or SAP IBP based weekly integration back to S/4HANA cloud.

So, yes, a workaround solution would be:

  • For you to set the material for weekly PIR planning and maintain weekly values directly
  • Or you use SAP IBP to generate and load weekly PIRs
  • If the forecast is still monthly, either split the file outside S/4 before you upload it or use SAP IBP to forecast and return weekly PIRs back to the Public Cloud.

Here are references for more guidance

2748331 - Maintain PIRs app doesn't show weekly/monthly PIRs - SAP for Me

3504764 - Splitting the monthly forecast data into equal quantities from Monday to Friday. - SAP for Me

3536168 - "Days" period indicator is not available in Maintain PIRs app - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: K6 Load Testing for SAP BTP (CAP + Fiori): Scope, CI/CD, and User Simulation?
entry.content:
Hi everyone, 

I’m currently getting familiar with testing in the SAP BTP ecosystem, since I'm working on a MTA app deployed on the Build Work Zone. I've started working with Wdi5 for the front and middle, and it seems K6 is the best option for load testing. 

Most K6 resources are generic, and I’m unsure how well they translate to SAP-specific architectures (XSUAA, destinations, OData, etc.). I have a few questions, which answers that would help me a great deal. 

1) What can reasonnably be tested with K6?

Should load testing target OData/CAP services directly or is there value in simulating flows through the Build Work Zone (E2E)? I'm assuming for now that backend-focused testing is more relevant, but maybe I’m missing some SAP-specific considerations.

2) Can K6 be integrated to a CI/CD pipeline in the same ways that Wdi5 can?

I’m using Azure DevOps with a standard MTA build/deploy pipeline. Should K6 tests run after deployment to a test environment, and are there common patterns for .yml configuration in this context?

3) What load models make the most sense?

K6 supports both Virtual Users and Requests per second. How can virtual users be tested? And should they even be tested?

In other word, should I simulate concurrent users or throughput against services? I suspect throughput-based testing may be more realistic, but I’d like confirmation.

4) User simulation?

Lastly - and this is the least clear part: how (and should I) mock users? If yes, what is the best method to do so? I'm thinking reusing a single OAuth token, since separate XSUAA auth seems very impractical.

Thanks for your guidance, and really looking forward any insight you might have!

Best, 

Theodore

entry.title: Enhancing Custom PS Report: Handling 1:N Relationship between WBS Element and Sales Orders (VBAP)
entry.content:
Hello
I am working on an enhancement for a custom Project System report (similar to a project overview/status report).

Current Situation: The report currently displays project details based on a WBS Element. However, the logic for fetching associated Sales Orders is currently limited to a 1:1 relationship. 

The Requirement: Our Project Managers often have multiple Sales Orders assigned to a single WBS element (e.g., one for services, one for materials, and one for an add-on).

  • Example Data in VBAP:

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113984

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113989

    • WBS: P.*******.1.3 -> SO: 113992

Currently, the report only reflects the first Sales Order. The goal is to update the report logic to display all associated Sales Orders for that single WBS number to provide a comprehensive revenue view.

Technical Questions:

  1. What is the best practice for displaying these multiple rows in an ALV-based report? Should I duplicate the WBS line for each SO, or use a "merged cells" approach in the layout?

  2. Are there any specific performance considerations when fetching all items from Table: VBAP for a large list of WBS elements?

  3. Besides VBAP , is there a more efficient standard SAP view or secondary index I should consider to find Sales Orders linked to a WBS?

Environment: SAP S/4HANA 

Any advice on the logic flow or standard Function Modules that handle this PS-SD link would be greatly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Increment counter function in sap build process autom...
entry.content:
Hello @liskaj01 ,

The initialize counter and increment counter functions aren’t suitable for your requirement.

  • Recommended approach: If you need to count loop iterations, use a forever or foreach loop—these provide an index value starting from 0, which you can use for counting.

  • Alternatively, you can create a number variable and increment its value by 1 within the loop to achieve the same result.

entry.title: Inquiry: Does SAP Build Work Zone Support CALM-Based Transport Configuration
entry.content:
Hello SAP Support Team,

I would like to seek clarification regarding transport management integration for SAP Build Work Zone.

Based on available documentation, SAP Build Work Zone currently supports transport movement primarily through SAP Cloud Transport Management Service (cTMS), which can optionally integrate with SAP Cloud ALM for deployment workflows. Multiple SAP Community and official SAP Help sources confirm the following:

  • SAP Build Work Zone objects (roles, catalogs, apps, sites, etc.) can be transported using cTMS, configured via service instances, destinations, and transport nodes in BTP subaccounts. 
  • cTMS integration can be linked with SAP Cloud ALM for deployment and transport orchestration, leveraging ALM for change tracking and landscape flow. [ainvest.com], [community.sap.com]
  • SAP Cloud ALM provides its own transport management support (for S/4HANA and other systems), but the documentation does not expli

entry.title: Re: Unable to set property from an XML using the XPath.
entry.content:
The entry is in a namespace, but the XPath prefix wasn't assigned.

XPath in CPI does not select default namespaces by default.

Prefixes must be defined in CPI (in Content Modifier → XPath):

Namespace mapping:
atom → http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom 
d → http://schemas.microsoft.com/ado/2007/08/dataservices


The right XPath
/atom:entry/d:fileExtension/text()

Please try this way.

Thanks,

Akhilesh

entry.title: ECS broke our RISE PCE WAF 2 and ECS cannot fix it
entry.content:
After 4 years in RISE PCE (Azure) we finally were requested by SAP to raise a ticket with ECS to migrate from WAF v1 to WAF v2.

it looks like ECS have hand crafted the configuration - its not via a defined terraform, bicep or arm template.  the WAF v2 rules are in restricted mode and nothing really works...

Fiori tiles won't work;  icon image are missing

ECS won't provide an export of the WAF config so we can compare and review - even though its our tenant and a shared security model.

you will have to forgive my ignorance in that as RISE PCE the WAF should have a defined security template that doesn't need any tweaking.

we already know SAP don't follow MS best practice for SAP on azure; or i would have had WAF v2 4 years ago.

entry.title: Re: French e-invoicing & S/4HANA Cloud: partial vs residu...
entry.content:
Hi Corinne,

Payment are either reported as CDAR 212 status for e-Invoicing or as flow 10.2/10.4 (B2B International/B2C) for e-Reporting when it is related to service (except for Incoming Down Payment).

As such, it is usually related to Company submitted to Deferred VAT. Even long before the upcoming French e-invoicing and e-reporting reform, SAP always recommended to use Partial Payment in this case to have an accurate transfer of Deferred VAT to VAT On Payment (the residual item would clear the full Open Item and recreate one, leading to incorrect VAT amount).

It means that the CDAR will be created for the amount partially paid (e-Invoicing). About e-Reporting, it is also the partial incoming payment that will be send.

The way you manage partial payment does not change in SAP with the upcoming e-Invoicing/e-Reporting. You will keep posting your document the same way and thus, no specific guidance is needed here.

Regards

Alexandre

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance #France #eInvoicing

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Compensation - Move employee's compens...
entry.content:
Did you check this box in the update; Apply the above selected updates to completed compensation worksheets as well. By default, update only applies to in-progress worksheets.? The completed forms would not appear on the salary tab of the current manager because they were not part of the workflow and therefore were never owners of the form. They would not see them in the completed folder.. Executive Review is based on RBP and should allow the new manager to see the records in their current state. I would consult the client as to why they cannot use executive review. As to why some show and some don't, there are a bunch of reasons why this would occur based on data and timing of transactions/updates. You would have to investigate each scenario further.

It seems like the system is working as designed but there are a lot of things happening in your scenario.

entry.title: Re: User not able to share bookmarks in sac stories
entry.content:
Dear saurabhverma,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

For this scenario, it is also worth checking whether this user is able to share other objects, such as story or model, with other users in this SAC system. In "Share Model" or "Share Story" dialog, whether the user list can appear as expected?

If the user list does not appear as expected in any Share dialog, it is recommend reviewing the help document Privileges and Permissions | SAP Help Portal.. This document provides detailed guidance on permissions required to view user lists in such dialogs.

As outlined in the document, the Read permission for the User object is essential to access and display the list of users. This permission allows you to see users in various scenarios, such as when selecting individuals to share a story with or adding members to a team.

To ensure the user has the appropriate permissions, it is therefore recommended to verify whether this user, who shares the object in question, has been granted at least "Read" access to the "User" object. This can be checked within the settings of the role assigned to the user. 

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Smaller ABAP Runtime Size Now Available in SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Starting March 31, 2026, SAP BTP ABAP environment introduces a new runtime sizing option: 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU) which is 8 GB of runtime memory.

With the introduction of 0.5 ACU, customers can now operate ABAP systems at 50% lower ABAP runtime cost compared to the previous minimum size of 1 ACU making it significantly more cost-efficient to run development, test, and sandbox systems.

This enhancement allows SAP BTP ABAP environment to run on smaller runtime units,  lowering the entry barrier for development and test systems.

What is an ABAP Compute Unit (ACU)?

ABAP Compute Units (ACUs) define the runtime capacity of an SAP BTP ABAP environment system. One ACU corresponds to 16 GB of memory allocated to the ABAP runtime. The total runtime size determines how much capacity is available across all ABAP application servers. For more information, see the documentation on ABAP Compute Units.

What’s New?

With this update SAP BTP ABAP environment now supports 0.5 ABAP Compute Units (ACU) as the new minimum system configuration.

It allows to run smaller systems, enabling more granular runtime sizing compared to previous configurations. It creates an additional option to better match the actual workloads, particularly for smaller environments.

What are the Key Benefits?

  • 50% lower ABAP runtime cost compared to the previous minimum size (1 ACU)
  • Improved resource utilization for smaller or variable workloads
  • Lower entry barrier for new and existing customers

For a detailed breakdown of cost components, see my previous blog post on ABAP environment cost calculation.

How to Adjust Existing Systems?

Adjusting the ABAP runtime size (ACUs) is handled via manual scaling and does not lead to a system downtime . You can use the SAP BTP Cockpit to update the service instance, for example, from 1 ACU to 0,5 ACU, see the documentation on Updating an ABAP System.

What Needs to be Considered?

For systems with a runtime size of 0.5 ACU, only one application server is used. This setup is ideal for development and test, but not recommended for productive use, as it may increase the likelihood of short outages.

Overview about typical use cases and recommend systems sizes

Use Case

Size

Total Runtime MemoryApplication Servers

Development, test, sandbox systems for smaller projects

0.5 ACU (new)

8 GB1 á 8 GB
Development, test, sandbox systems for bigger projects;
Productive systems for up to 1,000 active users per day		1 ACU16 GB2 á 8 GB
Productive systems with higher concurrency or up to 100 tenants 2 ACU32 GB4 á 8 GB
Productive systems with bigger max session size4 ACU64 GB2 á 32 GB
Productive systems with higher load in multitenant SaaS solution 4 ACU64 GB8 á 8 GB

 

Further References 

For more information about sizing options and configuration parameters in SAP BTP ABAP environment, see the documentation on Creating an ABAP System.

entry.title: Critical error in @sap/cds-dk + @sap/hdi-deploy (handlebars)
entry.content:
Hello,

We cannot deploy our application to our environments because the npm dependency audit is throwing a critical warning:
handlebars  4.0.0 - 4.7.8
Severity: critical
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection in CLI Precompiler via Unescaped Names and Options - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xjpj-3mr7-gcpf
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion when passing an object as dynamic partial - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xhpv-hc6g-r9c6
Handlebars.js has Denial of Service via Malformed Decorator Syntax in Template Compilation - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-9cx6-37pm-9jff
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion by tampering @partial-block - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3mfm-83xf-c92r
Handlebars.js has JavaScript Injection via AST Type Confusion - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2w6w-674q-4c4q
Handlebars.js has Prototype Pollution Leading to XSS through Partial Template Injection - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-2qvq-rjwj-gvw9
Handlebars.js has a Prototype Method Access Control Gap via Missing __lookupSetter__ Blocklist Entry - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7rx3-28cr-v5wh
Handlebars.js has a Property Access Validation Bypass in container.lookup - https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-442j-39wm-28r2
fix available via `npm audit fix`
node_modules/@sap/cds-dk/node_modules/handlebars


The problem seems to be that the latest version in the 8.9.x range (@sap/cds-dk 8.9.15) which we are using, has @sap/hdi-deploy 5.6.1 as dependency. This version of @sap/hdi-deploy has the package handlebars as a dependency, which was updated recently to version 4.7.9.
Please update the @sap/hdi-deploy package so that it uses handlebars 4.7.9, and update @sap/cds-dk to a new version that uses the updated @sap/hdi-deploy as dependency

entry.title: Re: TDS Adjustment Entry and impact on the TDS report
entry.content:
Hi all,

 

Please note that if TDS has been deducted incorrectly or if there is a need to change the applicable tax rate, the recommended approach is to reverse the original document and repost it with the correct details.

Alternatively, adjust the TDS amount by passing a rectification entry through the ''Enter Journal Voucher'' application, where applicable.

The above approaches ensure proper correction of TDS values while maintaining accurate accounting records and audit compliance. It is advisable to review the impact on related documents (such as invoices or payments) before performing any reversal or adjustment.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance UAE: Electronic Invoice in Utilities using Extensibility
entry.content:
You want to enable your system to create electronic documents for invoices posted in Utilities using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance. In this blog, we’ll show you an example on how this can be achieved using extensibility.  

Before you begin ensure that you have implemented the SAP Notes listed in 3705171 - Electronic Document Processing for United Arab Emirates - Peppol: Implementation Overview. You can also refer the SAP Help Portal for more information, see Electronic Document Processing.
Ensure you have also implemented the SAP Notes listed in 2736880 - eDocument EU PEPPOL: Overview note for IS-U.

Steps to Create Electronic Documents

  • Create eDocument Class
    1. Create 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class by inheriting CL_EDOCUMENT_EU_ISU class.
    2. Add the CONSTRUCTOR method in the ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU eDocument class to map the 'Z' eDocument IS-U class name to the field ms_edocument-edocument_class. 
    3. Redefine the method DETERMINE_PROCESS to assign the processes AEINV and AEINVIN.
    4. Use the table TFK003EDOC to configure eDocument type against the accounting document type.
    Example:

    Country/Region 

    Application Area 

    Document Type 

    eDocument Type 

    IN 

    Utility Company 

    FA 

    AE_INV 

     5. Add the class CL_EDOC_PROCESS as Friends of 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU' eDocument class. 

    Sample Code for CONSTRUCTOR and DETERMINE_PROCESS method:  
    Constructor method signature: 

     


METHOD constructor.
 super->constructor( io_source_data                 = io_source_data
                      iv_edoc_guid                   = iv_edoc_guid
                      iv_land                        = iv_land
                      iv_update_task                 = iv_update_task
                      iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor = iv_generic_badi_filter_adaptor
                      iv_simulate_for_email_option = iv_simulate_for_email_option ).

  ms_edocument-land                   = 'AE'.
  ms_edocument-edocument_class        = 'ZCL_EDOCUMENT_AE_ISU'.
ENDMETHOD.
METHOD determine_process.
    CASE ms_edocument-edoc_type.
      WHEN 'AE_INVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process = 'AEINVIN'.
        ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        CASE ms_edocument-interface_type.
          WHEN 'AIF_PROXY' OR 'STANDARD'.
            ms_edocument-process = 'AEINV'.
*           reference process is 'EUINV'
            ms_edocument-process_version = '0001'.
          WHEN OTHERS.
            MESSAGE e162(edocument) INTO gv_error_txt.
            raise_edoc_exception( ).
        ENDCASE.
    ENDCASE.
ENDMETHOD.

 

  • Create Factory Class
    1. Create a 'Z' factory class to get the instance of eDocument IS-U class by inheriting CL_EDOC_FACTORY class and redefine its method CREATE_EDOC_INSTANCE. 
    Example: ZCL_EDOC_FACTORY_AE_ISU
    2. Configure this 'Z' eDocument factory class in the view EDOFACTORYV using the transaction SM30.

    Source Type  

    Factory Class  

    ISU_INVOIC  

    ‘Z’ factory class name (your factory class that you just created)  


    Sample Code:
METHOD create_edoc_instances.

    DATA: lv_land_iso      TYPE edoc_land,
          lv_error_txt     TYPE string,
          ls_source_header TYPE edoc_src_header,
          lo_edocument     TYPE REF TO zcl_edocument_ae_isu.

    CLEAR: lv_land_iso,
           lv_error_txt,
           ls_source_header,
           lo_edocument,
           et_edocument_obj.

* Country
    ls_source_header = io_source->get_header( ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'COUNTRY_CODE_SAP_TO_ISO'
      EXPORTING
        sap_code  = ls_source_header-land
      IMPORTING
        iso_code  = lv_land_iso
      EXCEPTIONS
        not_found = 1
        OTHERS    = 2.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE e881(b1) WITH ls_source_header-land INTO lv_error_txt.
    ENDIF.
    IF lv_land_iso <> 'AE'.
      RETURN.
    ENDIF.

    CREATE OBJECT lo_edocument 
      EXPORTING
        io_source_data = io_source
        iv_update_task = iv_update_task.

    APPEND lo_edocument TO et_edocument_obj.

  ENDMETHOD.

  • Mapping Class
    1. We have a generic mapping class CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL_ISU. If you need to enhance the mapping logic, you can use the EDOC_ADAPTOR BAdI's method SET_OUTPUT_DATA. For more details, refer to Enhancements for Electronic Documents.
    Alternatively, you can implement your own custom mapping class. 
    2. Configure your mapping class in the view EDOMAPCLASSDETV using the transaction SM30. 
    Example:

    eDocument Process 

    Version 

    Interface ID 

    eDocument Type 

    Source Type 

    Mapping Class 

    EUINV 

    1 

    UBL_21_INVOICE_REQUEST

    AE_INV 

    ISU_INVOIC 

    <Your Z mapping class or CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL_ISU> 

    EUINV

    1

    UBL_21_INVOICE_RESPONSE

    AE_INV

     

    CL_EDOC_MAP_UBL

     

  • Maintain the Required Customizations
    Apart from maintaining Master Data, Customizing and Integration as explained in the SAP Help Portal, you must also configure the company code for the following IS-U source type.

    1.  Activate company code for ISU_INVOIC source type in the view EDOCOMPANYACTIV for eDocument creation.
    Example:

    Company Code  

    Source Type  

    Application Active  

    <Your company code>  

    ISU_INVOICE  

    X  


By following these steps, you can efficiently create electronic documents for invoices in the utilities sector, ensuring compliance and enhancing operational efficiency. 

Don't forget to test it thoroughly. 

We hope this blog post was useful for you! You can leave a comment here or continue browsing our community for more blog posts on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance topics. 

Looking forward to seeing you here again!

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
@RubikWal 

I really appreciate having someone like you to challenge my perspective from different angles!

DataStore isn't specifically tailored for JMS. It works well alongside AMQP.  AMQP is excellent for the initial call and handling transient errors via automatic retries. However, for more permanent errors - where individual processing (whether automatic, semi-automatic, or manual) is required - I recommend using DataStore as the storage layer for a Dead Letter Queue.

Yesterday another fantastic tool came out for DataStore management, pls have a look:
https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/cloud-integration-pipeline-datastore-extension-ui/ba-p/14326020 

Feel free to send me DM on LinkedIn so we can continue this discussion outside the comments section
BR, Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Good day @SAPPS 

Thank you for your question.

The simple best practice I can recommend is as follows:

  • Keep the executing project in Company B
  • Staff the employee from Company A to the relevant work package in that project
  • Let the employee record time against the staffed assignment using Manage My Timesheet

  • Process the posted time (through Process Unposted Time Confirmations) and, where needed, initiate intercompany billing from the sending company to the receiving company.

 

Regarding your Scenario is: An employee of a Company A wants to book timesheets in a Project getting executed in Company B.

Yes, that is the standard business design, provided the employee is staffed correctly and the intercompany charging design is in place. The whole purpose of resource related intercompany billing is that one company code provides services for another company code.

 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/29cf986299714386847f4d4c9bb86994/21218854c46ee75ee10000000a44176d.html?locale=en-US

2870580 - Inter Company Time Recording is not allowed for Unstaffed Projects - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Best Practice for Intercompany Project Timesheets in...
entry.content:
Dear @SAPPS,

Recommended/proposal process steps:

1. The project should be created in the receiving company and moved to In Execution status. SAP documents that once the project is in execution, resources can book time to it via Manage My Timesheet. If stricter control is required, set Restrict Unstaffed Posting = Yes, so only staffed resources can post time to the project

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

2. The employee should be assigned as a project resource, ideally with a hard-booked assignment. SAP documents that a hard booking is the confirmed assignment status and, once hard-booked, the staffing information is updated immediately. SAP also describes intercompany project execution as a process where one company delivers services for another company project and confirms that project management supports staffing resources across companies

More details below:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/analyzing-business-process-steps-and-apps-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-resource-management

https://learning.sap.com/courses/exploring-sales-intercompany-processes-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/understanding-intercompany-billing-for-cross-company-cost-accounting-postings_da2dcbc8-0efa-4042-91ba-b7531f866d2c

3. After staffing, the project appears automatically in the employee My Tasks area in Manage My Timesheet and the employee can post hours directly to that project/work package. SAP further states that once time is booked and approved where applicable, it is reflected on the project as both a time and cost posting

4. If approval is part of the process, submitted time entries are routed to My Inbox for review and approval. SAP documents this as the standard approval mechanism for project-related time entries

More details below for points 3 and 4:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/managing-projects-and-resources-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-professional-services/managing-time-recording-and-approvals

5. For the delivering company, SAP documents a standard flow consisting of:

  • creation of the intercompany sales order as a one-time setup for each delivering company involved
  • execution of Generate Intercompany Billing Request using the job template Resource-Related Billing Between Company Codes
  • creation of a DMR3 debit memo request containing the time recordings and expenses and
  • creation of the final intercompany invoice, after which SAP can automatically create the mirrored accounts payable document in the receiving company

Summary:

In Public Cloud the recommended best practice for this scenario is to keep the employee in Company A as the delivering resource, keep the project in Company B as the receiving company, staff the Company A employee to the Company B project/work package and let the employee record time there using Manage My Timesheet. The resulting cross-company cost posting is then settled through the standard resource-related intercompany billing process. SAP explicitly documents that applications such as time recording for projects or services can create cross-company cost accounting documents and that for these documents the required legal invoicing is supported through the intercompany billing process, with the receiving company payable created automatically

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: sac的年龄计算有问题，目前是出生日期当天年龄就+1，和实际不符合&#...
entry.content:
Hi Jimmy,

Hope you are well. Thank you for your message.

From a standard international age perspective, the calculation is:

  • Birthdate: 2001‑01‑31
  • Reference date: 2026‑01‑31
  • When someone reaches their birthday in a given year, they turn a year older.
  • On 2026‑01‑31, Chen Daxin’s birthday arrives, so he/she turns 25.

Therefore, 25 is mathematically correct for international age (full-year age).

If it is expected age to be "international age-1" when the Month and Day are the same for the Birthday and Reference date, it is required to create an additional calculation in story. For example, the calculation can be defined as below:

IF([d/"Mdoel A":Birthday].[p/MONTHDESC]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/MONTHDESC]and[d/"Model A":Birthday].[p/DAY]=[d/"Model A":Reference date].[p/DAY] ;[#Date Difference ]-1 ; [#Date Difference])

Thank you for your time.

Best wishes,

Lena

entry.title: Re: The Data Ownership dilemma — and how SAP Business Data Cloud Data Products helps resolve It
entry.content:
Thank you for your comments.

Here is what I can tell you :
@Anthony : Data Products are functionnaly quite similar to the BW Business Content..
But what changes is that BW wan only focusing on ERP data while Data Products focus on ALL SAP data : S/4, Concur, SuccessFactors, Ariba ... AND non-SAP data, leveraging technology standards such as SQL language,  spark ...
And, we also deliver out of the box reporting (Intelligent Applications). This was not available in former BWs

@nachtaktiv : this relies on the concept of Clean core. Most of the processes can be standardized, and SAP was implemented to match these standard processes. Data products reuse all the BusinessProcess that is available out of the box within SAP applications. If customers need to modify/adapt these standard process, then indeed, IT will work on the modified processes. But in most cases, 80% remains standard.

entry.title: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently working on a project where SAP ECC 6.0 is already implemented. At present, I am involved in a Chart of Accounts (COA) restructuring activity within the same system, with a focus on Asset Accounting.

The client requirement is to assign new GL accounts to existing assets. To achieve this, I have created new asset classes, configured account determination, and assigned new GL accounts in AO90.

Now, the client expects that the balances from the old asset masters—including accumulated depreciation—should be transferred to the new asset masters. Additionally, the system should calculate and display planned depreciation values period-wise in the asset master.

To address this, I have taken the following steps:

  1. Created legacy assets under the new asset class using AS91, mirroring the existing assets.
  2. Used AS92 to take over values by entering acquisition value and accumulated depreciation.

While the net book value is appearing correctly, the planned depreciation is not being calculated as expected. Instead of distributing depreciation period-wise, the system is showing the remaining depreciation as a lump sum in the next period.

Could you please suggest the correct steps or procedure to meet this requirement?

Thanks in advance

FIN Asset Accounting 

@Lakshmipathi @Divyesh_Mistry

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
I'm not sure how else to explain it except to give an example.

A monthly paid employee has a regular monthly contribution to 401k of $3,000 (pre tax).  They also have a monthly contribution to 401k roth of $2,750 (after tax).  According to the IRS the annual contribution max for 401k (pre and post tax) is $24,500.  This person is a high earner (annual FICA wages over $150,000).  They are 55 years old so they are eligible for an additional $8,000 in catchup.  Which must be done with an after tax roth plan.  Reminder they are already contributing to a roth plan.

In July the employee reaches the annual max of $24,500 for regular contribution for both plans.  They are eligible for an additional $8,000 in catchup.  Instead of doing 100% of the catchup to the roth plan it is doing the following.

$1,300 regular 401k (this is fine because that is what was needed to meet the max)

$1,700 regular 401k catch up pre tax (which is what I'm questioning, it should be 401k roth after tax catch up.

$0.00 to 401k roth after tax (again, I think this is fine because 1,300 from the 401k regular pre tax met the annual max)

$2,750 401k roth catch up after tax

I would expect to see $4,450 ($1,700+2750) deduction for 401k roth after tax catch up and $1,300 to regular 401k (to meet the max).  

Note - if the employee just has regular 401k and they have all of the criteria listed above, their catch up is automatically rolled over to roth as it should.  So that configuration is correct.  We are only seeing this if the employee has both a 401k pre tax and and 401k roth after tax.

Note - it is behaving this way for 457 plans as well.

Impacto social: Além de todo o aprendizado, sua inscrição ajuda uma causa nobre!

Uma Comunidade Global, Agora em Goiânia!

O SAP Inside Track não é apenas um evento. Ele é um movimento global que fortalece profissionais, incentivando a troca de conhecimento de forma acessível e colaborativa.

Seja você um profissional experiente, iniciante ou entusiasta do universo SAP, esse evento é para você!

entry.title: What should enterprise teams learn from the recent Axios supply chain incident?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I wanted to start a broader discussion about dependency trust and supply chain risk after the recent Axios incident, where compromised npm releases were reported for axios 1.14.1 and 0.30.4. Public reporting says those versions were maliciously published, and the Axios GitHub issue tracked the incident as it unfolded. StepSecurity also reported that the malicious releases were removed from npm and that the latest dist-tag reverted to 1.14.0.

What makes this especially concerning is that Axios is not some obscure package. It is a very widely used dependency, directly or indirectly, across many enterprise stacks. That means even if your own project does not explicitly add Axios, you may still inherit that risk through frameworks, SDKs, or platform libraries used in corporate environments. The npm page for @sap/cds shows how central SAP’s CAP tooling is in many Node.js-based enterprise projects, which makes this kind of dependency-chain discussion very relevant for SAP developers too.

For me, this raises a few important questions:

  • Are we still too quick to auto-update dependencies just because they come from popular packages?
  • Should enterprise teams introduce a waiting period before adopting freshly published versions of critical libraries?
  • How much visibility do we really have into transitive dependencies used by frameworks and vendor packages?
  • Should vendors and platform teams communicate more clearly when widely used dependencies are involved in incidents like this?

In SAP-related projects, this topic feels even more important because many teams rely heavily on trusted platform libraries and may assume that popular dependencies are inherently safe. But incidents like this show that popularity is not a security guarantee. A single compromised release in a common package can affect a very large ecosystem in a very short time.

I would be interested in hearing how others handle this in practice:

  • Do you pin versions more aggressively now?
  • Do you block newly released packages for some time before adoption?
  • Do you audit transitive dependencies regularly?
  • Should enterprise vendors like SAP be more conservative about dependency updates after incidents like this?

I think this is a good moment for all of us to revisit how much trust we place in open-source dependencies, especially in enterprise software landscapes.

 

Fonts: 

https://github.com/axios/axios/issues/10604 (Official)

https://www.stepsecurity.io/blog/axios-compromised-on-npm-malicious-versions-drop-remote-access-trojan

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
You have done the right groundwork with AS91/AS92, but the issue you are seeing with depreciation not spreading period‑wise is tied to how legacy values are taken over in Asset Accounting. In ECC, when you use AS92 to enter accumulated depreciation, the system interprets this as “already posted depreciation” rather than “planned depreciation base.” That’s why it lumps the remaining depreciation into the next period instead of recalculating the full schedule.

So what you can do is recreate one test asset under the new class, carefully set the capitalization date and depreciation start date, enter acquisition and accumulated depreciation in AS91/AS92, then run AFAB. In AW01N, you should now see depreciation distributed period‑wise.

entry.title: Integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning seamlessly with SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I’m sharing this blog after extensive effort, research, and hands-on analysis. I hope it helps anyone looking to integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning (LMS) while leveraging a custom domain through SAP Build Work Zone.

 

Requirement

When accessing the SAP SuccessFactors system, SAP provides a standard URL like:

https://hcm22.sapsf.com/login?company=<company name>

The SF Home page will look like as below:

 

 

After logging in, users are directed to the SuccessFactors dashboard as above. From there, navigating to the Learning Management System (LMS) opens another SAP-hosted URL:

https://<company name>.lms.hr.cloud.sap/learning/user/deeplink.do?fromSF=Y&linkId=HOME_PAGE

 

Challenge

Both URLs use SAP’s default hostnames. However, based on client requirements, access should be through a custom domain, such as:

https://hrhub.abcservices.abc.com

 

Solution Overview

To achieve this, we integrate SAP SuccessFactors with SAP Build Work Zone, where the Custom Domain Service is already configured.

For a detailed, end-to-end guide on Custom Domain Service, refer to my other blog:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/custom-domain-service-in-sap-btp-build-work-zone-standard-edition/ba-p/14314511

 

Note: This setup uses SAP Build Work Zone – Standard Edition, but the same approach also works with the Advanced Edition.

 

Analysis & Implementation Steps

After thorough analysis, the following approach worked successfully:

Step 1: Create Destination in SAP BTP

Create a destination in the BTP subaccount pointing to the SuccessFactors system:

Destination Name: hrhub

 

Step 2: Configure Work Zone Content

In SAP Build Work Zone, create:

  • A local application which will be having the destination as "hrhub" and the App UI Technology will be Dynamic URL and Relative Path to App will be /sf/home

    here, we can also add one more app which we can show in the work zone and access the above app by following:

     

  • Page: Below will be the page where we added the App

  • Space: Add the page in the space:

     

  • Role: Add the space and app in the role so it will be visible to user:

     

     

Step 3: Assign Roles

  • Assign the role to the Work Zone site

     

  • Assign the role to users in the BTP subaccount

     

Step 4: Access via Custom Domain

You can now access SuccessFactors directly using the custom domain:

https://hrhub.abcservices.abc.com/site/workzone-std-prd?sap-ushell-config=headerless#sf-display

 

As per the above Screen shot, the SF Home page is opening through work zone URL and Dashboard is similar which we have seen when accessing directly through SF.

Now we can access the LMS by clicking on tile "View My Learning" or from the top Menu "Learning"

In the above URL, it is not changing the URL when accessing the LMS, the URL is remaining same as custom domain url which show the customer host instead of SAP Standard URL.

Common Issue & Fix

Issue

While navigating between SuccessFactors and LMS, you may encounter the following browser error:

SecurityError: Failed to read a named property 'document' from 'Window':
Blocked a frame with origin "https://<company name>-dev.lms.hr.cloud.sap"
from accessing a cross-origin frame.

Refused to display 'https://<company name>-dev.lms.hr.cloud.sap/'
in a frame because it set 'X-Frame-Options' to 'sameorigin'.

The above error will come first time when accessing the LMS, if we refresh the page then this error might go. In our case this error was coming when accessing first time and after refresh the page it was not coming. Below is the error screen shot:

 

 

Resolution of the above Error:

Apply the following SAP Note in LMS:

SAP Note 2169861 –
"Learning Plan Internet Explorer error 'This content cannot be displayed in a frame' or blank page in Chrome or Firefox"

Configuration Change in LMS Side

Navigate to:

System Admin > Configuration > System Configuration > WEB_SECURITY

Set the following property:

clickjackingProtection.enabled=false

⚠️Important: Disabling clickjacking protection should be evaluated carefully based on your organization’s security policies.

 

Summary

This guide outlines the key steps to integrate SAP SuccessFactors and SuccessFactors Learning with SAP Build Work Zone using a custom domain. This approach enhances user experience by providing a seamless and branded access point while maintaining centralized navigation.

I hope this helps streamline your implementation and reduces dependency on standard SAP URLs.

If you have any questions or run into issues, feel free to reach out.

Regards,
Rohit Gera

entry.title: Load Crystal Report data from order without save draft
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We recently updated our Feature Pack from 2108 to 2511 (yes, I know that's a significant jump). After this update, our Crystal Report layout — used in order drafts — stopped reflecting unsaved changes in real time. Previously, any modifications made to an order draft were immediately visible in the Crystal Report preview, even before saving. Now, we need to save the draft first in order to see the changes reflected in the report.

My question is: did this behavior change intentionally in one of the Feature Packs between 2108 and 2511? Is saving the draft now a requirement by design, or is there a workaround to restore the previous behavior?

Any help would be appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: CX Onboarding: How to Begin Your Journey with SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
How to Begin Your Journey with SAP Enterprise Service Management

 SAP Enterprise Service Management   provides a unified, omnichannel approach to service delivery, leveraging AI-powered case and inquiry management to ensure efficient user interactions and timely resolutions. ESM extends service delivery beyond traditional customer support to encompass internal service processes for employees, suppliers, and partners, standardizing and automating service processes across departments while aligning with their specific workflows to drive organizational efficiency. ESM is not a standalone product. It's an extension of SAP Service Cloud Version 2 that requires both the initial setup and configuration of Service Cloud V2 first.

This architectural relationship ensures that ESM leverages the core functionalities of Service Cloud V2 (such as Agent Desktop, Case Designer, Solution Finder, and Collaboration) while adding specialized capabilities including Self-Service, Forms, Integration Scenarios, and Business Documents. ESM includes AI tools like Case Summary for automatic summaries, an Email Draft Recommender that significantly reduces drafting time, and Similar Case Recommendation for identifying relevant past cases. These features enhance first-call resolution and increase shared service productivity. The solution also includes embedded analytics enabled by the SAP Analytics Cloud solution and standard reports, yielding advanced operational insights.

From an onboarding perspective, we understand that customers benefit from learning the essentials early. This reduces wait times, allows teams to focus on implementation and configuration, and accelerates time‑to‑value. To support this, we’ve created a guided onboarding journey that introduces foundational SAP concepts and the core elements of your solution, following a natural path from Get Started all the way to Prepare to Go Live.

Below you’ll find how to begin working with SAP Enterprise Service Management, using our dedicated Onboarding Resource Center and its curated content.

 

Step 1: Getting access to your solution

Access begins on the contract start date defined in your agreement. On this date, the nominated IT Contact will receive a welcome email with instructions on accessing your SAP Enterprise Service Management systems. At this point, environments can be provisioned, and the initial configuration phase can begin.

Read our ESM Provisioning Guide

 

Step 2: Discover our free Onboarding Course

Upon completing this course, you'll gain a comprehensive understanding of the customer onboarding journey, ensuring a smooth implementation of your SAP Enterprise Service Management solution.

You'll learn how to access SAP for Me and the SAP Enterprise Service Management instances and obtain both technical and non-technical support. The course will teach you how to effectively plan projects using the SAP Activate Methodology, Cloud ALM, and Best Practices. You'll become familiar with product release cycles, explore the SAP Enterprise Service Management solution and its enablement resources, and discover how to harness AI and innovation for business success. Finally, you'll learn to refine your cutover strategies, monitor systems with advanced tools, and align with SAP's strategic road map to develop a future-ready, high-performance solution. 

Access the course here: Cloud Onboarding for SAP Enterprise Service Management.

 

Step 3: Gearing up for the implementation

After gaining access to your system, it’s important for the project team to become familiar with SAP essentials such as contacting support, navigating SAP for Me and its dashboards, and other key operational basics. We offer a dedicated playlist covering these areas.

We also encourage you to explore our Onboarding Resource Center, which follows the full onboarding journey. After reviewing the Get Started with SAP content, you can continue to Plan Your Implementation, where you’ll learn how to get cloud‑ready (especially if moving from on‑premise), how to manage change effectively within your organization, and how to apply our implementation methodology, SAP Activate.

Explore this course: Implementing SAP Enterprise Service Management.

 

Step 4: Extend your SAP Enterprise Service Management Knowledge

In our Onboarding Resource Center, you can also explore our guides, including:

  • Customer Use Cases: explore customer use cases across Human Resources, Finance, Procurement, Supply Chain, and Facility Management.
  • Data Migration: understand how to prepare, transform, and load data into SAP Enterprise Service Management using a structured five‑step migration approach.
  • Features & Functions, and AI Guides: familiarize yourself with the key features and functions of your solution, including its AI capabilities.

You can also explore Foundational resources for SAP Enterprise Service Management in the SAP Community.

Be sure to share these resources with your implementation partner and internal teams.

Thank you for reading this blog. We hope you found the content insightful. Please give us a like or leave a comment; we’d love to hear from you!

 If you’re starting your SAP Enterprise Service Management project, you can request a one‑on‑one call with one of our onboarding advisors.

 Want to get in touch with us? Send an email to SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

 

 Key Resources

  • Onboarding Resource Center
  • SAP Community blog: How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices
  • ESM on Product Portal page – What is Enterprise Service Management?
  •  ESM on SAP Help Portal 
  • Solution Guide for ESM 
  • SAP Enterprise Service Management Features | SAP Help Portal
  • What's New in SAP Enterprise Service Management | SAP Help Portal
  • Introducing Enterprise Service Management (ESM) – Short Introduction to Enterprise Service Management (ESM) and its core principles.
  • Stay Informed SAP Sales and Service Cloud Cloud Newsletter covering ESM
  • SAP Discovery Center Business AI Features

 

Watch: SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR | Product Inspiration Series

Watch this product inspiration video to learn about the strategic direction and importance of SAP Enterprise Service Management for HR scenarios as well as our major innovations as part of the Intelligent CX portfolio.

entry.content:
